question:
Will Melbourne City FC win on 2025-10-25?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for October 25, 2025
If Melbourne City FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.a-league.com.au/

evidences.title: ALW SQUAD UPDATE: City farewells four Premiership winners
evidences.content: Melbourne City Home News # ALW SQUAD UPDATE: City farewells four Premiership winners Communications Department 5 September, 2025 ## Share ### Share this link via ### Share this: ### Or Copy Link Melbourne City FC can confirm that Tyla-Jay Vlajnic, Isabella Accardo, Emilia Makris and Kiera Meyers have departed the Club ahead of the new season. Returning to City in September 2024 after two years away, Vlajnic made 27 appearances last season and brought up her 100th A-League Women’s appearance in the process. A key contributor in the Club’s Premiers Plate triumph – her fifth piece of silverware in City colours – she departs with 82 Club appearances to her name, the fifth-highest tally in City Women’s history. [...] Accardo spent four seasons with the Club, totalling 13 appearances, and was a member of City’s back-to-back Premiership-winning squads. Makris departs the Club by mutual termination after having made nine appearances last season. Meyers originally joined from Southern United in February 2023 and went on to feature 14 times in City colours, including three starts. Melbourne City FC Director of Football, Michael Petrillo, said: “TJ was a key member of our squad last season...

evidences.title: Report finds significant improvements in A-League discipline, VAR decisions - Friends of Football
evidences.content: Yellow cards per game dropped 12.8%. Red cards fell by 56.8%. Second yellow card dismissals were down 13%. Rates of red cards for Serious Foul Play and Denial of Goal Scoring Opportunity remained stable, showing consistent application of laws in high-impact incidents. The referee development pathway also saw “a notable boost” with 16 new match officials making their debuts in refereeing, assistant refereeing and VAR roles, up from only two newcomers the previous season. Jon Ross. “This season’s data demonstrates the progress we’re making in delivering officiating that is both accurate and aligned with the modern game,” Football Australia’s interim chief executive Heather Garriock said. [...] “To see improved decision-making across the board, combined with fewer cards and a low VAR intervention rate, speaks volumes about the quality and consistency of our match officials.” Football Australia’s head of referees Jon Moss said: “We’re seeing tangible year-on-year improvement — not just in accuracy, but in game management and the integration of new talent into the elite level. “The data tells a clear story: fewer cards, fewer incorrect calls, and more consistency. This is a credit to...

evidences.title: Closing line: The most important metric in sports trading | by Trademate Sports | Medium
evidences.content: drops to 2.30. If we look at the vig-free odds (removing Pinnacle’s margin of 2%), the odds is 2.346 (2.30 \ 1.02). The odds at Ladbrokes remains at 2.50, meaning that there now exists an edge in the market of 6.56% [ ((2.50 / 2.346)-1)\100 ]. The market movement from 2.68 to 2.30 is a fluctuation of 16.5%. For Pinnacle odds to swing the other way and eliminate the edge, it is going to take new information. This information must then convince bettors to place hundreds of thousands of dollars on a draw or away win to change the odds. Now there is only 1 hour before kick-off so we can assume that the probability of this occurring is rather low. This implies that what is an edge 1 hour before kick-off is also likely to remain an edge at the time of kick-off. Or in other words if we had [...] Through looking at the correlation between the expected probability of a game’s outcome and the actual outcome it is possible to determine how accurate a bookmaker’s odds are. From asample size of 397,935 football games offered by Pinnacle Sports, a sharp bookmaker, there existed a high correlation (r-squared = 0.997) between the closing lines and the observed probabilities. In other words, Pin...

evidences.title: Pinnacle Odds Comparison | Sharp Lines & Best Odds | JediBets
evidences.content: #### Step 1: Use Pinnacle as the "True Line" Since Pinnacle has the lowest-hold lines in the world, their odds represent the market's best estimate of true probability. When recreational books deviate from Pinnacle, that's where value exists. #### Step 2: Compare Across Multiple Books Sharp bettors compare Pinnacle against softer books like: FanDuel & DraftKings: Often 0.5-1 point off on NFL spreads BetMGM & Caesars: Slower to adjust to line movement Prop Markets: Massive discrepancies (5-10%+ edges possible) #### Step 3: Calculate Your Edge Example: Pinnacle has Chiefs -3 (-110) → 52.38% implied probability DraftKings has Chiefs -2.5 (-110) → Better line, same juice You're getting an extra 0.5 points = +EV opportunity #### Step 4: Bet the Deviation [...] ### Beating Pinnacle's Closing Line: The Holy Grail In sports betting, there's one metric that matters above all: Closing Line Value (CLV) relative to Pinnacle. Why Pinnacle's Close? It's the most efficient line in the world - represents true market consensus What is CLV? If you bet Chiefs -2.5 and Pinnacle closes at -3.5, you gained 1 point of CLV Why it matters: Consistent positive CLV vs Pinnacle = long-term profitability Re...

evidences.title: Law 12 - Fouls and Misconduct
evidences.content: The yellow card communicates a caution and the red card communicates a sending-off. Only a player, substitute or substituted player or team official may be shown the red or yellow card. [...] The referee has the authority to take disciplinary action from entering the field of play for the pre-match inspection until leaving the field of play after the match ends (including penalties (penalty shoot-out)). If, before entering the field of play at the start of the match, a player or team official commits a sending-off offence, the referee has the authority to prevent the player or team official taking part in the match (see Law 3.6); the referee will report any other misconduct. A player or team official who commits a cautionable or sending-off offence, either on or off the field of play, against an opponent, a team-mate, a match official or any other person or the Laws of the Game, is disciplined according to the offence. [...] Team Officials Where an offence is committed by someone from the technical area (substitute, substituted player, sent-off player or team official) and the offender cannot be identified, the senior team coach present in the technical area will receive the san...

evidences.title: Global study of penalty cards in professional football
evidences.content: The study highlights important differences in the number of warnings and sending offs according to country. It also reveals the existence of significant correlations between the amount of cards per match and many socio-economic indicators for nations: the gross domestic product, the human development index, the homicide rate and an indicator for perceived corruption. Figure 1:study sample ## 2. Penalty card differences between countries On average, referees handed out 4.42 yellow and 0.25 red cards per match for the 101,491 fixtures analysed in the 87 leagues taken into account. The teams playing away received the majority of cards: 53.0% of the yellow and 56.7% of the red ones. Cards were mainly handed out in the second half: 65.8% for the yellow and 81.8% for the red ones. [...] Figure 2: global statistics for penalty cards Figure 2: global statistics for penalty cards The number of cards varies considerably according to the confederation of belonging of the leagues studied. At one extreme, in South America (CONMEBOL), referees handed out on average 5.83 cards per match. This figure is 45% higher than that observed in the 15 Asian (AFC) leagues studied (4.00). Invariably, away...

evidences.title: Free Sports Betting Odds | Lines, Spreads & Scores
evidences.content: ## The 3 Odds Formats You’ll See Most sites let you choose your preferred display. ### American odds (US) -110 means you risk $110 to profit $100 +150 means you risk $100 to profit $150 Implied probability Negative: odds / (odds + 100) Positive: 100 / (odds + 100) ### Decimal odds Decimal includes your stake in the return. Example: 2.50 $100 × 2.50 = $250 return ($150 profit) Implied probability: 1 / 2.50 = 40% ### Fractional odds Fractional shows profit relative to stake. Example: 9/2 Risk $2 to profit $9 (return $11) Implied probability: 2 / (9 + 2) ≈ 18.2% ## Common Line Types (Quick Reference) ### Moneyline Pick the winner—no spread. Example: Mets -210 / Pirates +180 ### Point spread A handicap that creates a fairer price. [...] ### Point spread A handicap that creates a fairer price. Example: Team A -4 (-110) vs Team B +4 (-110) Win by exactly 4? That’s a push. ### Totals (Over/Under) A line on the combined score. Example: Total 44 Final combined 47 → Over wins Final combined 41 → Under wins Player or team stats (yards, shots, strikeouts, etc.). Great for targeted opinions, but compare price and limits carefully. ### Futures Long-term markets like season wins or a title win...

evidences.title: Fouls and misconduct (association football) - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Yellow cards are less common, though a typical game will feature a few – at the 2014 FIFA World Cup there were, on average, about three cautions per game. Dismissals are much rarer; that same tournament saw an average of 0.2 red cards per match. ## Referee's discretion [...] ### Yellow card (caution) A yellow card is shown by the referee "Referee (association football)") to indicate that a player has been officially cautioned.: Law 12.3 The player's details are then recorded by the referee in a small notebook; hence a caution is also known as a "booking". A player who has been cautioned may continue playing in the game; however, a player who receives a second caution in a match is sent off (shown the yellow card again, and then a red card). Law 12 of the Laws of the Game "Laws of the Game (association football)") lists the types of offences and misconduct that may result in a caution. Players can be cautioned and shown a yellow card if they commit the following offences: [...] There are also two offences which apply in matches using the video assistant referee system: What constitutes cautionable unsporting behaviour is generally at the referee's discretion, though Law 12 lists...

evidences.title: Betfair Exchange Value Betting Strategy for 6% Yield
evidences.content: ## Why Smart Betting Club rated Pinnacle Odds Dropper a “strong buy” Smart Betting Club’s 2025 review reaffirms Pinnacle Odds Dropper as a top data-driven betting tool with strong ROI, CLV, and long-term profitability. ## Pikkit Pro review 2025 Pikkit Pro ($39.99/mo) upgrades the free bet-tracking app with CLV analysis, odds alerts, SGP line shopping, web access, exports, and promo tracking. Built for serious bettors, but pricey—best suited for those wanting pro tools without enterprise costs. ## Subscribe to The Steam Report Stay ahead of the betting markets with The Steam Report - your monthly digest of advantage betting insights, industry news, and user results from the sharpest minds in betting. Smart betting icon Smart betting icon [...] ## Risk Management# He uses Pinnacle Odds Dropper (POD) primarily as a risk management tool. "If I have a bet live on an exchange, I'll set up alerts around that market to ensure that if Pinnacle moves their line, I have time to cancel my exchange bets and not get picked off." ## The Strategy's Edge# The approach has proven particularly effective when properly calibrated to market liquidity. evidences.title: Law 5 - The Referee | IFAB
evidences.content: has the authority to take disciplinary action from entering the field of play for the pre-match inspection until leaving the field of play after the match ends (including penalties (penalty shoot-out)). If, before entering the field of play at the start of the match, a player commits a sending-off offence, the referee has the authority to prevent the player taking part in the match (see Law 3.6); the referee will report any other misconduct has the power to show yellow or red cards and, where competition rules permit, temporarily dismiss a player, from entering the field of play at the start of the match until after the match has ended, including during the half-time interval, extra time and penalties (penalty shoot-out) [...] The referee has the authority to take disciplinary action until leaving the field of play. Even though the match has ended, an appropriate card may still be shown if it helps manage the situation or calm the player. Verbal dissent is punished with a caution (yellow card), unless the words are offensive, insulting, or abusive (red card). Regardless of whether the card is shown or not, the referee must report the offence to the appropriate authorities. The r...
